note: Protected Pages stores protected paths in its own DB table (no config entity), guards them via a response event subscriber that redirects to a /protected-page password form, and keeps text/password/session settings in the protected_pages.settings config object. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source for; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the Protected Pages module, where in the admin UI do I add a new password-protected path, and what are the field requirements for the path?
evals.expected_output: Add one at Admin > Configuration > System > Protected Pages > Add (/admin/config/system/protected_pages/add). The relative path must start with '/', e.g. /node/5, and may use '*' wildcards like /new-events/* or /* for the whole site; you also set a per-page password.

evals.prompt: Using Protected Pages, how do I password-protect an entire section of the site (all pages under /members) with one rule?
evals.expected_output: Add a protected page with the path /members/* — the '*' wildcard matches all pages under that path (the subscriber uses the core path matcher). /* protects the whole site.

evals.prompt: In Protected Pages, what happens when an anonymous visitor requests a protected path, and which mechanism enforces it?
evals.expected_output: A response event subscriber (ProtectedPagesSubscriber on KernelEvents::RESPONSE) detects the protected path and redirects the visitor to the /protected-page password form (with the page id and a destination). Entering the correct password unlocks it for the session.

evals.prompt: Which Protected Pages permission lets a trusted role skip the password prompt entirely and view all protected pages directly?
evals.expected_output: The 'bypass pages password protection' permission — the subscriber returns early for users who have it, so they are never prompted.

evals.prompt: How do I control how long a visitor stays unlocked after entering a Protected Pages password before being prompted again? Give the config key and its default meaning.
evals.expected_output: Set the session expire time (in minutes) at the settings form, key password.protected_pages_session_expire_time on protected_pages.settings; default 0 means unlimited (unlock lasts the whole session).

evals.prompt: In Protected Pages, how do I make one single global password unlock every protected page instead of a separate password per page?
evals.expected_output: On the settings form set the Global password setting (password.per_page_or_global) to 'only_global' and set the global password; then only the global password is accepted for every protected page. 'per_page_or_global' accepts either.

evals.prompt: I want to add a Protected Pages rule programmatically in Drupal. Which service and method do I use, and how is the password handled?
evals.expected_output: Use the protected_pages.storage service (ProtectedPagesStorage) and call insertProtectedPage(['password'=>..., 'title'=>..., 'path'=>...]); the password must be pre-hashed with the core 'password' service. Protected pages are DB rows, not config entities.
